hero.subtitle: Professional outfitting for international hunters. From the volcanic plateaus to the alpine peaks of the Southern Alps.

story.title: Our Story
story.missionText: "Founded to introduce international hunters to the quality of New Zealand trophies and the raw majesty of its wild landscapes."

alex.bio: With over 40 years of experience in the international hunting industry, Alex is a veteran who understands the importance of heritage and ethics. He has guided hundreds of successful hunts across the globe and brought his expertise to the pristine landscapes of New Zealand.

artem.bio: Artem brings 15 years of dedicated hunting experience, combining technical precision with a deep passion for the wild. As a co-founder, he ensures that every detail of the safari experience meets the highest standards of luxury and authenticity.

vuk.bio: With more than 35 years in the field, Vuk brings deep knowledge of New Zealand terrain, animal behaviour, and the patient, fair-chase pace required for successful trophy hunting.

fairChase.label: Fair Chase
fairChase.text: Every hunt is earned, physical, and respectful of the animal and the land.

ethicalStandard.label: Ethical Standard
ethicalStandard.text: Shot selection, recovery, and camp conduct are our non-negotiable baseline.

precisionPlanning.label: Precision Planning
precisionPlanning.text: Itineraries shaped around your trophy goals, species windows, and preferred terrain.

territory.title: Where You Hunt
territory.subtitle: Programs built around the right country first - matched to the species, the seasonal window, and the pace your group wants in the field.

north.name:
Kaimanawa
Central Plateau
north.terrain: Beech and podocarp bush, river valleys, and tussock tops.
north.signature: The only wild sika herd in the Southern Hemisphere. The Kaimanawa and Kaweka country delivers the defining North Island bush hunt across deep cover, river systems, and open tops.
north.permit: DOC public-land permits plus private-land permission where required.

access.label: Clements Mill Road
access.note: Main recreation hunting access east of Taupo.

access.label: Southern Corridor
access.note: Turangi side approach for selected catchments.

access.label: Kiko Road
access.note: Useful upper-country entry into bush and tops.

south.name:
Southern Alps
Alpine Country
south.terrain: Alpine grasslands, sub-alpine scrub, steep faces, and remote basins.
south.signature: DOC places tahr across the Southern Alps chain, while chamois range through South Island high country and forest margins. This is real alpine work, not a scenic add-on.
south.permit: DOC access, concessions where required, and weather-led field planning.

access.label: Helicopter Support
access.note: Used only when terrain and weather truly demand it.

access.label: Foot and 4WD
access.note: Primary access into most productive basins and faces.

access.label: Alpine Camps
access.note: Field camps extend reach for longer mountain programs.

docPermitted.label: DOC Permitted
docPermitted.text: Valid DOC permits used for all public-land access.

terrainFirst.label: Terrain First
terrainFirst.text: Programs matched to country and seasonal window.

flexibleAccess.label: Flexible Access
flexibleAccess.text: Context-led selection of foot, 4WD, or helicopter.

species.title: Target Species
species.subtitle: Our programs focus on mature, hard-antler trophies and prime winter coats across both North and South Island territories.
species.footerText: All hunts follow fair-chase principles. Multiple-species itineraries can be built by arrangement - final programs shaped by trophy goals, seasonal timing, and the right country.

animals.name: Red Deer
animals.headline:
The Classic
NZ Roar
animals.description: Classic New Zealand roar hunting for mature stags in beech bush, open hill country, and alpine basins. The autumn roar window runs from late March into April - vocal, territorial stags at their most aggressive.

animals.name: Sika Deer
animals.headline:
Kaimanawa
Signature
animals.description: The only wild sika herd in the Southern Hemisphere. The Kaimanawa whistle period runs late March into May - elusive bush deer, dense cover, and one of the most authentic stalking experiences in New Zealand.

animals.name: Himalayan Tahr
animals.terrain: Alpine grasslands, remote basins, steep faces
animals.headline:
The Alpine
Trophy
animals.description: Genuine mountain work in the Southern Alps. Winter coat quality and rut timing make May-July the premium window. Steep basins, remote access, and the most physically demanding hunt in the program.

animals.name: Fallow Deer
animals.headline:
Fallow
Rut
animals.description: Targeting mature, palmate bucks during the croak. Fallow provide a different challenge, often holding tight in mixed cover and defending specific rut pads.

stay.title: Premium Lodging
stay.subtitle: Whether operating from our Ohakune base or high-country backcountry huts, comfort and recovery are built into every itinerary.

lodges.name: Ohakune Lodge
lodges.tagline: Modern comfort on the edge of the Kaimanawa.
lodges.description: Our primary headquarters for North Island operations. Fully equipped for comfort between bush missions, featuring premium dining, gear rooms, and immediate access to both public and private blocks.
lodges.highlight: Strategic base for Sika and Red Deer operations.

lodges.name: Alpine Hut
lodges.description: Functional, weather-proof, and positioned deep in Tahr and Chamois country. These huts strip away the unnecessary, focusing entirely on warmth, recovery, and proximity to the game.
lodges.highlight: True backcountry immersion with helicopter access.

gallery.title: Field Gallery
gallery.subtitle: A visual record of our operations across New Zealand. Honest representation of the terrain, the animals, and the reality of the hunt.

contact.title: Plan Your Hunt
contact.subtitle: Every program is custom-built. Submit your details or contact us directly to begin discussing seasonal windows, species goals, and logistics.
contact.formDetails: Additional Details (Group size, preferred dates, etc)
contact.successMsg: Inquiry received. We will contact you shortly.
contact.errorMsg: Error sending message. Please try again or use direct contact.

footer.tagline: Professional fair-chase outfitting for international hunters across New Zealand.
footer.copyright: Kaimanawa Trophy Safaris. All rights reserved.
